stories.headline: Commerce Department finalizes new AI chip export rules
stories.summary: The Department of Commerce released final rules expanding restrictions on the export of advanced AI accelerators to a broader list of countries. Industry groups raised concerns about implementation timing.

perspectives.keyFraming: Centers on the technical scope of the rules and the industry reaction.
perspectives.summary: Mainstream tech coverage details the new license thresholds, the country tiering, and the 60-day implementation window. Industry trade groups have called for guidance on the 'unrestricted research collaboration' carve-out.

sources.title: US finalizes broader AI chip export controls

perspectives.keyFraming: Chinese state media frames the rules as US economic containment.
perspectives.summary: Chinese state outlets describe the rules as the latest in a multi-year US effort to constrain China's AI development. Coverage highlights commentary from Chinese officials calling the measures unilateral and damaging to global supply chains.

sources.title: US escalates chip-war with new export controls

perspectives.keyFraming: Official statement emphasizes national security framing.
perspectives.summary: The Department of Commerce statement says the rules 'protect critical US technology from being used to advance the military capabilities of adversaries' while preserving commercial relationships with allies.

sources.title: Commerce-DoD joint statement on AI export rule update
